title: Auction gate
does:
An agent integrates the Attestr auction gate by passing two things into mppx: gate.fetch and gate.onChallenge. You do not modify mppx itself.

gate.fetch is the HTTP entry point mppx uses. It records the last 402 response so a full payment bundle (challenge + HTTP context) can be assembled and sent to an auction or saved for review.

gate.onChallenge runs after a 402: it builds the bundle, and waits until terms are acceptable (in the demo: a JSON file stands in for a winning and accepted auction quote), then mppx continues and pays using the methods you pass in (same wallet here; production might route funds differently).

Integration is one call: Mppx.create({ fetch: gate.fetch, onChallenge: gate.onChallenge, methods: [...] }).

This demo is just for a Tempo integration, as we still don't have access to credit card/SPT processing from Stripe.

snippets.file: agent.mjs — plug the gate into Mppx.create
payment_bundle.mjs — createCapture402Fetch (how gate.fetch remembers the last 402)

title: Workspace + merchant server
does: The demo is initialized and prior run artifacts are cleared. The mock merchant server is started and the merchant's 'paid' endpoint is probed to ensure it's live.

title: Agent starts checkout
does:
The agent initiates and prints its setup (wallet, merchant URL, etc). It then issues a real mppx.fetch to the merchant.

In production, bundleOut will be the transaction terms from the merchant along with the agent/sponsor's identity. termsFile is the bid/funds from the issuing bank. For this demo they are just initialized here in lieu of actual identity and auction.

title: 402 + payment bundle (agent)
does: This shows the moment the agent has a 402 and has built the payment bundle of the parsed payment challenge (what the merchant is asking for: amount, asset, recipient, challenge id, chain context, etc.), plus HTTP context from the 402 (URL, method, headers / WWW-Authenticate). In production this would also include an agent or sponsor's identity/credit history

title: Wait for lender / auction terms (agent)
does: The agent polls MPP_AUCTION_TERMS_FILE until the file exists, parses as JSON, and the defaultAcceptTerms returns proceed (not wait/abort). In production MPP_AUCTION_TERMS_FILE is the terms returned when an auction selects a winning bid.

title: Lender / auction terms accepted (agent)
does: In production, terms from the winning bid or issuing bank reach the agent through Attestr integration. The agent only continues to pay when the accept policy allows it—in this repo, defaultAcceptTerms on the parsed terms JSON must return proceed. Those terms gate payment; mppx still settles against the merchant’s original Payment challenge, and the terms are not yet merged into the cryptographic proof.

title: Paid + final HTTP result (agent)
does: In agent.mjs, one call to mppx.fetch(paidUrl) finishes: mppx has already handled 402, payment, and the follow-up request with credentials. The agent then reads the final Response status, optional Payment-Receipt header, and response body, and emits a paid demo event with that snapshot.
